column7: Increases filled grains per panicle; nitrogen timing at heading is critical for grain plumpness.
Stimulates early root development; balanced NPK ensures vigorous establishment in irrigated fields.
Improves grain filling efficiency; sulfur promotes enzyme systems involved in starch synthesis.
Boosts filled grain ratio; K improves sink strength and assimilate remobilization to grains.
Enhances grain filling rate; biostimulant compounds improve assimilate mobilization from leaves to developing grains.
Bacteria component provides growth-promoting substances; combined with nano-K improves grain plumpness and weight.
Organic materials feed earthworms and soil fauna; improves soil physical structure for better root penetration.
Azolla biomass improves soil organic carbon; slow-release N from decomposition supports consistent hybrid growth.
Foliar K at reproductive stage maximizes grain filling; rapid absorption supports assimilate movement to developing grains.
